mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.5 percent, tight labor market conditions accelerate wage growth, compressing corporate profit margins and triggering negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production and residential housing starts signals robust aggregate demand outpacing supply constraints, driving earnings revisions upward and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with Rank IC > 0.05 in out-of-sample testing, or individual series ICs dominate the interaction term in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising rate environment, real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent annualized overrides discount rate headwinds by confirming strong corporate earnings, producing positive equity returns over a 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25 percentage points in 3 months
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when GDPC1 exceeds 2.5% during rate hikes, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects underlying economic momentum that sustains consumer spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical difference between positive and negative payroll growth months.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 2.5 percent year-over-year, inflation expectations embed into equity valuations as a hedge against currency debasement, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when CPI growth exceeds 2.5%, or the signal produces a negative Rank IC in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong payroll growth and stagnant industrial production indicates a services-led economic shift that boosts corporate margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve predictive power over a baseline model with only PAYEMS, or forward returns are flat when payrolls rise but INDPRO falls.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid industrial output expansion, a concurrent rise in the federal funds rate signals central bank confidence in economic strength rather than inflation fear, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises alongside FEDFUNDS, or the joint signal's Rank IC drops below 0.04 in walk-forward testing.
